tax: Placed at genus rank, Cinderling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a creeping low-oxygen smoulder body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a smouldering mat-body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dull-red glow-front in mat. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a grey ash-band through mat — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Cinderling occupies deep fungal mats. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a spark smoulders in low-oxygen mat and recur with fresh mat re-seeds the smoulder, sustaining themselves on slow combustion of mat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: creeps unseen and flares on air. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: smouldering body and ash-creep. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: hidden spread and re-ignition. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Cinderling matters because burns boots before it is seen. It lets survey reports sort creeping smoulder forms from leaping ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Cinderling is the genus-rank grouping of creeping smoulder elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a smokeless smoulder body that hides in mat and flares on air, and divide below into species groups by mat type and oxygen level. A hidden creeping fire that wakes to a draught.
